title: cerastium caespitosum f. glandulosum
intro: Cerastium caespitosum f. glandulosum, also known as the glandular mouse-ear chickweed, is a flowering plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic agent to prevent infection. Cerastium caespitosum f. glandulosum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
